title: Use breakpoints from the UI kit throughout the entire project and mark the project-specific breakpoints as deprecated

exploratory.charter: Risk charter — explore boundary conditions and integration points after BREAKPOINTS refactor
exploratory.notes: All 4 observations and 1 risk are pre-existing or environmental (Playwright MCP timing, Apollo stale cache, max-width clamp by design, sticky-element architecture). None caused by VCST-4517.

business_rules_note: No P0/P1 BL invariants are directly affected by a pure import-path refactor with identical breakpoint values. Implicit P2-UX 'responsive layouts engage at correct viewports' is verified.

exploratory.charter: Risk charter for VCST-4517 — boundary conditions after BREAKPOINTS refactor

verdict_reasoning: All 5 acceptance criteria pass. All 6 declared breakpoints (xs:480, sm:640, md:768, lg:1024, xl:1280, 2xl:1500) engage at correct pixel thresholds, verified via tailwind utility audit on chrome and firefox. Zero console errors mentioning BREAKPOINTS / ui-kit / core/constants across full smoke flow on both browsers. Theme bundle 2.49.0-pr-2201-4439-44393039 confirmed deployed (JS bundle + footer DOM). Boundary thrash and hydration at just-past-breakpoint widths produced no flicker or race conditions. Exploratory session surfaced 4 Observations + 1 Risk, all of which are pre-existing behaviors or environmental quirks unrelated to this refactor — no bugs to file. Verdict: PASS_WITH_NOTES because exploratory captured non-blocking observations; the refactor itself is clean.
